name: reliquary
summary: A small vault-chamber housing saintly relics, holy objects, or bound curiosities. Cool, close, lined with pedestals and cases. Sometimes unlit — the relics do not need to see.

primary_palette.note: a single great casket at the center, flanked by candles
a lone pedestal with a warded relic; the chamber is otherwise bare
the relic is a saint's body itself
several smaller reliquaries arranged along the walls — a collection

story_prompts: a relic missing — empty pedestal, wards cold, dust outline on the stone
sealed for a generation — cobwebs undisturbed, candles never lit
a cursed relic — wards carved thickly on every wall, chains across the casket
newly endowed — crates of wrapped items waiting to be catalogued
a pilgrim permitted rare entry — one lamp lit, a prayer written in chalk on the floor

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: either near-dark (0.1) with single focused candle, or entirely unlit — the relic's presence IS the lighting

anti_patterns: keep it small — a reliquary is a vault, not a gallery
pedestals hug the walls; the center should be clear for approach or kneeling
no seating and no furniture — relics are approached on foot, reverently
